stories.title: McCollum Does It Again — Hawks Walk Off Knicks 109-108, Lead Series 2-1

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
CJ McCollum. AGAIN.

Fadeaway jumper with 12.3 seconds left. Hawks 109, Knicks 108.

Atlanta leads the series 2-1 and McCollum is EATING in the clutch.

#HoopHeroes #ATLHawks #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Jalen Johnson was EVERYWHERE in Game 3.

24 pts. 10 reb. 8 ast.

Knicks had no answer. Hawks lead 2-1. New York needs to figure it out — FAST.

#HoopHeroes #ATLHawks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

CJ McCollum has officially become the Atlanta Hawks' playoff boogeyman — and the New York Knicks simply have no answer for him.

With 12.3 seconds left on the clock and Atlanta trailing by a point, McCollum caught the ball on the wing, faded away from his defender, and drained the jumper that gave the Hawks a 109-108 victory in Game 3. Atlanta now leads the first-round series 2-1.

The Knicks had battled back from early adversity. Atlanta opened the game with an 11-0 run and three consecutive three-pointers to close the first quarter. Jalen Brunson's three-point play with 1:03 remaining gave New York a 108-105 lead — and then McCollum answered.

Jalen Johnson was Atlanta's engine all night: 24 points, 10 rebounds, and 8 assists. Jonathan Kuminga added 21 points. For New York, OG Anunoby's 29-point effort and Brunson's 26 weren't enough. Karl-Anthony Towns chipped in 21.

Game 4 is Saturday in Atlanta. The Knicks need a win or they return home staring at a 3-1 deficit.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: McCollum Does It Again: Hawks Edge Knicks 109-108, Lead Series 2-1

stories.title: Raptors Erupt 126-104 — Barnes and Barrett Drop 33 Each, Wolves Feed Off 47-23 Run

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Barnes 33. Barrett 33. 19 straight scoring possessions. 47-23 run.

Toronto came ALIVE in Game 3. Raptors 126, Cavs 104. Series: CLE leads 2-1.

#HoopHeroes #TorontoRaptors #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Two stars. 33 points each. 9-for-13 combined from three.

Barnes and Barrett carried Toronto in Game 3. Raptors are ALIVE in this series.

#HoopHeroes #Raptors #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

Down 2-0 and playing in front of a home crowd that needed a reason to believe, the Toronto Raptors delivered one of the most stunning performances of the 2026 playoffs.

Scottie Barnes and RJ Barrett each scored 33 points — combining on 23-for-36 from the field and 9-for-13 from beyond the arc. And when the game was in doubt, the Raptors simply refused to stop scoring.

Toronto went on a 47-23 run by scoring on 19 consecutive possessions spanning the third and fourth quarters. That kind of sustained efficiency is nearly impossible to defend, and the Cavaliers had no answer. The final score, 126-104, wasn't close enough to reflect how completely the Raptors owned the second half.

Off the bench, Collin Murray-Boyles added 22 points and eight rebounds. Jamison Battle went 5-for-5 for 14 points. Cleveland still leads 2-1, but Toronto has the momentum now. Game 4 is Sunday.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Barnes and Barrett Drop 33 Each as Raptors Destroy Cavaliers 126-104, Cut Series to 2-1

stories.title: Edwards Exits With Knee Scare, But Wolves Roll Nuggets 113-96 — Minnesota Leads 2-1

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Wolves WIN 113-96 and lead the series 2-1.

But all eyes are on Anthony Edwards, who left Game 3 in the fourth quarter with a knee injury. He was already questionable. Updates to come.

#HoopHeroes #MNTimberwolves

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Jokic reportedly shot 7-for-26 in Game 3. SEVEN for TWENTY-SIX.

Wolves held Denver to 11 points in Q1. Minnesota leads 2-1 — Nuggets in crisis.

#HoopHeroes #MNTimberwolves #DenverNuggets

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Minnesota dominated Denver in Game 3, winning 113-96 to take a 2-1 series lead. But the bigger story isn't the win — it's what happened to Anthony Edwards in the fourth quarter.

Edwards was listed as questionable before tip-off with right knee maintenance, played 24 minutes and scored 17 points on 6-for-15 shooting before heading to the locker room without returning. He was visibly in discomfort. This is a continuation of a knee issue that caused him to miss 11 of the Timberwolves' final 14 regular season games.

The good news for Minnesota: the team didn't need him to seal the victory. Ayo Dosunmu came off the bench for 25 points and 9 assists. Jaden McDaniels finished with 20 points and 10 rebounds. Donte DiVincenzo added 15 points and 4 steals.

The Nuggets were the problem. Nikola Jokic reportedly shot 7-for-26 from the floor, and Minnesota outscored Denver 68-34 in the paint while holding the Nuggets to just 11 first-quarter points — a franchise postseason record. Denver built nothing against Minnesota's defense.

The full impact of Edwards' injury will be clearer before Game 4. But Minnesota has proven it can win without him at full strength.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Wolves Dominate 113-96, Lead Series 2-1 — But Anthony Edwards Exits With Knee Injury

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
THREE games tonight that matter:

No. 1 Thunder vs. Suns G3 — 3:30 PM NBC (OKC leads 2-0)
Celtics vs. 76ers G3 — 7 PM Prime (series 1-1)
Lakers vs. Rockets G3 — 8 PM Prime (LAL leads 2-0)

What are you watching? #HoopHeroes #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 2:30 PM ET

Friday night in the NBA playoffs is packed, with three first-round games that could significantly shift the postseason landscape.

First up: No. 1 Oklahoma City Thunder host the Phoenix Suns in Game 3 at 3:30 PM ET on NBC. The Thunder enter leading the series 2-0, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander operating at back-to-back MVP level. The Suns are without a win and without Jalen Williams, who remains out with a hamstring injury.

At 7 PM ET on Prime Video, the Boston Celtics travel to Philadelphia for Game 3 against the 76ers in a series knotted at 1-1. This is the most unpredictable matchup of the round.

Finally at 8 PM ET on Prime Video, the Lakers visit Houston for Game 3 with a 2-0 series lead. Austin Reaves is listed as questionable (oblique). LeBron James put up 28 points, 8 rebounds, and 7 assists in Game 2. Kevin Durant is also questionable for the Rockets with an ankle issue.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Friday Playoff Triple-Header: Celtics-76ers, Thunder-Suns, Lakers-Rockets All on Tap

stories.title: Jokic's 7-for-26 Nightmare — Nuggets Face Elimination Without Star-Level Support

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nikola Jokic was unrecognizable in Game 3.

7-for-26 from the floor. Denver got outscored 68-34 in the paint.

Minnesota leads 2-1. Can Jokic bounce back and save the Nuggets?

#HoopHeroes #DenverNuggets #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Window: 4:00 PM ET

It was not the Nikola Jokic anyone expected to see in the playoffs.

In Game 3, Jokic reportedly shot 7-for-26 from the field as Denver fell 113-96 and dropped to a 2-1 series deficit. It was a performance completely at odds with his regular season dominance — the three-time MVP rendered inefficient while Minnesota smothered everything Denver ran in the half-court.

It's not the first time Minnesota's defense has gotten the better of Jokic this series. In Game 2, he finished with 24 points, 15 rebounds, and 8 assists but went just 2-for-12 in the fourth quarter when the game was on the line. The Timberwolves' switching defense and physicality disrupt his rhythm in ways most teams cannot.

Denver also got outscored 68-34 in the paint — Jokic's primary domain — and allowed Minnesota to build a 27-point lead in the third quarter.

Denver's path forward is complicated further by Anthony Edwards' knee injury. If the Wolves have their full arsenal available, the Nuggets will need Jokic to find his vintage form immediately. Denver's season depends on it.

#HoopHeroes | #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Jokic's Worst Playoff Night in Memory Puts Nuggets in Crisis — Down 2-1 to Minnesota
